steps.title: Write the three triangle inequality conditions
steps.content:
For a triangle with sides 10, 12, and $x$, the sum of any two sides must exceed the third side. This gives us three conditions:
  • $10 + 12 > x$ (the sum of the known sides exceeds the unknown)
  • $10 + x > 12$ (one known side plus the unknown exceeds the other)
  • $12 + x > 10$ (the other known side plus the unknown exceeds the first)

steps.title: Solve and combine the inequalities
steps.content:
Simplifying each inequality:
  • $10 + 12 > x$ becomes $x < 22$
  • $10 + x > 12$ becomes $x > 2$
  • $12 + x > 10$ becomes $x > -2$ (redundant since $x$ must be positive)
The binding constraints are $x > 2$ and $x < 22$. Combining these gives $2 < x < 22$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
